summary: The author developed a deep-learning-guided program synthesis system that operates within a tight search-and-learn loop, actively searching program space and adapting at test time using test-time training via hindsight relabeling. Experimental evidence indicated that combining search with learning outperforms pure search methods when both are constrained to the same number of predictions per task. However, the research remained incomplete and failed to solve any of the private test tasks. Ultimately, the highest leaderboard score was achieved by applying minor adaptations to last year's transduction with test-time training approach.
extensive_summary:
## Approach Overview
The core of this work focuses on a deep-learning-guided program synthesis system designed for the ARC25 challenge. Rather than relying on static models, the author implemented a tight search-and-learn loop where the system actively searches through program space and dynamically adapts during inference. This adaptation is driven by test-time training (TTT) utilizing hindsight relabeling.

## Key Findings and Limitations
Through experimentation, the author gathered evidence that the combination of search and learning yields better performance than pure search approaches when both are constrained to the same computational budget (predictions per task). Despite these promising comparative results, the search-and-learn pipeline was not fully completed and did not successfully solve any of the private test tasks.

## Final Submission Strategy
Due to the incomplete state of the primary research direction, the author's best leaderboard performance was actually secured by making minor adaptations to a previously successful method: last year's transduction with test-time training approach. The writeup serves primarily as a technical report summarizing these explorations and directs readers to an external blog for further details.
competition_flow: The pipeline centers on a deep-learning-guided program synthesis system that searches program space and adapts via test-time training with hindsight relabeling, though the final submission relied on a minor adaptation of last year's transduction with test-time training approach.
insights: Search combined with learning outperforms pure search approaches when constrained to the same number of predictions per task.
